# Multi-Statement Processing Review: PEPES MEXICAN RESTURANT

## Executive Summary
- ISO: Clover / First Data
- Date range: 2024-11 - 2024-12
- Statements analyzed: 2
- Total volume: $109,634.41
- Total fees: $2,726.05
- Average effective rate: 2.49%
- Effective rate trend: stable
- Benchmark status: within competitive range (1.95% - 2.45% for Restaurant / Food Service at this volume)
- Pricing model: interchange_plus
- Headline projected annual savings: $1,313.22

## Effective Rate Trend
- 2024-11: 2.50% on $53,291.02 volume and $1,330.96 fees
- 2024-12: 2.48% on $56,343.39 volume and $1,395.09 fees

## Operational Context
No material zero-volume, refund, card-mix, or prior-period adjustment issues were detected from the available parsed data.

## Dispute Trend
- Direction: none
- Total dispute costs: $0.00

## Fee Change Timeline

## Top Findings
1. MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED may be avoidable through configuration or qualification fixes: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). PCI compliance validation has not been completed. Complete the PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ). Once validated, this fee is eliminated or significantly reduced. Cumulative impact $99.90; projected annual impact $599.40; action: Complete PCI/security validation or correct the terminal/gateway setup.; difficulty: no negotiation required.
2. ACCESS FEE is charged at the same rate across independent networks: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). This pattern suggests a processor-controlled access fee may be applied uniformly across independent networks. Ask for pass-through documentation showing the source and exact wholesale basis. Cumulative impact $119.79; projected annual impact $718.74; action: Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented.; difficulty: investigation required.
3. BENTOBOX ONLINE ORDER FEE is a third-party service fee: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). This appears to be a third-party online-ordering service charge. Verify that the service is active, wanted, and priced correctly. Cumulative impact $34.65; projected annual impact $207.90; action: Verify the third-party service is active and cancel or renegotiate it if it is not needed.; difficulty: investigation required.
4. DISCOVER AUTH FEE exceeds the reference rate: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). This is a per-authorization fee charged by the processor. It is applied to every card transaction and is negotiable. If multiple per-auth fees are stacked, the total per-authorization cost may be higher than competitive rates. Cumulative impact $5.56; projected annual impact $33.36; action: Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented.; difficulty: investigation required.
5. MC LICENSE VOLUME FEE exceeds the reference rate: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). This is a Mastercard acquirer license fee assessed as a percentage of volume. It is a network pass-through fee, but the rate varies by acquirer and is not publicly fixed at a single rate. If the rate exceeds commonly cited industry references, request documentation from your processor. Cumulative impact $3.69; projected annual impact $22.14; action: Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented.; difficulty: investigation required.
6. Per-authorization fee is above competitive benchmark: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). This is processor-controlled per-authorization pricing. The finding measures potential savings from reducing the per-auth cost to the competitive benchmark target. Cumulative impact $70.66; projected annual impact $423.96; action: Negotiate the processor-controlled rate.; difficulty: negotiation required.
7. MONTHLY ADVANTAGE FEE MCVDB adds hidden percentage markup: Appeared on 2 analyzed statement(s). This appears to be a processor-controlled percentage markup embedded as a monthly advantage fee. It is negotiable and should be separated from true card-network pass-through costs. Cumulative impact $29.71; projected annual impact $178.26; action: Negotiate the processor-controlled rate.; difficulty: negotiation required.
8. Other minor rate exceedances: 4 lower-dollar finding(s) are included in savings and action totals but not shown individually. Combined projected annual impact is $11.37. Cumulative impact $1.90; projected annual impact $11.37; action: Review as part of the broader pass-through documentation request.; difficulty: investigation required.

## Recurring Avoidable Fees
- MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED: $49.95/month, present on 2 statement(s), $99.90 paid across analyzed periods, $599.40/year projected. PCI compliance validation has not been completed. Complete the PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ). Once validated, this fee is eliminated or significantly reduced. Action: Complete PCI/security validation or correct the terminal/gateway setup. Difficulty: no negotiation required.
- BATCH SETTLEMENT FEE: $8.85/month, present on 2 statement(s), $17.25 paid across analyzed periods, $106.20/year projected. This is a processor-controlled per-batch settlement fee. It is charged each time batches are closed or settled and is negotiable. Ask whether batching can be consolidated or whether the per-batch charge can be waived. Action: Ask whether batching can be consolidated or the per-batch settlement fee can be waived. Difficulty: negotiation required.
- VI TRANSACTION INTEGRITY FEE: $0.45/month, present on 2 statement(s), $0.90 paid across analyzed periods, $5.40/year projected. Visa transaction failed CPS qualification requirements. Check terminal or gateway configuration. Ensure all required data elements (AVS, CVV2) are submitted. Action: Complete PCI/security validation or correct the terminal/gateway setup. Difficulty: no negotiation required.

## Master Narrative
Across the two statements from 2024-11 through 2024-12, the biggest cumulative cost issue is the processor-controlled and avoidable security validation charge, MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED. It appeared on both analyzed statements and totaled $99.90, with a projected annual impact of $599.40. This fee is tied to PCI compliance validation not being completed, so the practical fix is to complete the PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire or correct the terminal/gateway setup so the charge is eliminated or significantly reduced.

A second set of costs appears to be tied to processing setup and network pass-through questions. ACCESS FEE was charged on both statements and totaled $119.79; the reviewed notes say the pattern suggests a processor-controlled access fee applied uniformly across independent networks, so pass-through documentation should be requested. DISCOVER AUTH FEE and MC LICENSE VOLUME FEE also exceeded reference levels on both statements, with cumulative impacts of $5.56 and $3.69, respectively. These items are grouped with other pass-through documentation items because the data shows they may not be priced at transparent wholesale basis. The combined annual savings tied to this documentation review are $780.21.

The monthly statement also shows processor-controlled pricing above competitive levels in two places. The per-authorization fee was above the competitive benchmark on both statements, with a cumulative impact of $70.66 and projected annual savings of $423.96 if brought down to benchmark. In addition, MONTHLY ADVANTAGE FEE MCVDB adds a hidden percentage markup and totaled $29.71, with $178.26 in projected annual savings. These are negotiation items rather than validation items, because the fees are described as processor-controlled and negotiable.

The effective rate was stable over the period, moving from 2.50% in 2024-11 to 2.48% in 2024-12, and the average across the statements was 2.49%. The data indicates the change was driven by both volume and fee mix rather than a single dramatic shift, with total volume of $109,634.41 and total fees of $2,726.05 across the period. The merchant is within the competitive range for Restaurant / Food Service at this volume, so the opportunities below are optimization items rather than a broad benchmark failure.

Two additional items round out the main opportunity list. BENTOBOX ONLINE ORDER FEE appeared on both statements and totaled $34.65; it is described as a third-party online-ordering service charge, so it should be confirmed as active and wanted before any further action. BATCH SETTLEMENT FEE also appeared on both statements and totaled $17.25; it is described as a processor-controlled per-batch settlement fee that may be reduced by consolidating batching or waived through negotiation. A smaller set of minor rate exceedances is also included in the savings totals, with $1.90 cumulative impact and $11.37 annual impact, and there is no gap month in the reviewed period.

Prioritized action items, in the order provided, are: 1) Complete PCI/security validation or correct the terminal/gateway setup, expected annual savings $604.80, difficulty no negotiation required; 2) Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented, expected annual savings $780.21, difficulty investigation required; 3) Verify the third-party service is active and cancel or renegotiate it if it is not needed, expected annual savings $207.90, difficulty investigation required; 4) Negotiate the processor-controlled rate, expected annual savings $602.22, difficulty negotiation required; 5) Ask whether batching can be consolidated or the per-batch settlement fee can be waived, expected annual savings $106.20, difficulty negotiation required. If all actions are taken, total projected annual savings are $2,301.33.
